About this restaurant

  • Good for special occasions
  • Charming
  • Good for groups
Join the flock. Dine on eclectic shareables, imbibe an ever-changing drink list, and groove to a diverse vinyl collection in a buzzing service driven space on 17th Avenue.

Experiences

  • Nocturnal Pigeons

    1 - 8 people

    Multiple dates available
    NOCTURNAL PIGEONS is Pigeonhole 17th’s late-night indulgence for those who prefer their evenings a little… stimulated. A cheeky nod to after-dark cravings (the culinary kind, of course), this elevated snack menu features build-your-own seafood platters, buck-a-shuck oysters, and half-priced bottles of wine that tend to send the night in slightly unhinged directions. Available 9pm-10pm Tuesday-Thursday, 10pm-1am Friday-Saturday. Reservations available, walk-ins encouraged!

Offers

  • Late Night Vibing

    1 - 8 people

    Multiple dates available
    Fri - Sat - 10:00 PM - 12:30 AM
    Indulge on our limited late night food menu, featuring Chilled Seafood, Fried Goodies and a Sweet Treat. Crush some Martinis and other delectable items during our buzzing and boisterous late night offering. Available on Friday and Saturday evenings after 10:00pm. Kitchen closes at 12am.

Review summary

AI-generated from verified diner reviews
Pigeonhole on 17th Ave offers an exceptional dining experience with a diverse menu and standout service. Guests rave about the "fantastic brunch" and "amazing food and service." While the "creative menu" and "delicious plates" impress, some find the music too loud. A "fun yet elegant" atmosphere makes it a must-visit.
1,873 Reviews
  1. P

    Patricia Gold

    Calgary47 reviews

    5.0

    Dined 4 days ago

    1. Overall 5
    2. Food 5
    3. Service 5
    4. Ambience 5
    Had such a fantastic brunch yesterday. I love that Pigeonhole offers more choices at brunch beyond the typical egg-centric lineup at most other places. The service was outstanding - from the hostess who greeted me at the door, walked me to my table and checked my coat to our server Lewey who was super attentive throughout our entire dining experience. Hospitality lives in the fine details and this team absolutely nailed it yesterday! Special props to the team member who thoughtfully packed my leftovers so that nothing got soggy and I could reheat each component of the dish separately to enjoy it the next day. Those small touches matter, and they never go unnoticed.

    Is this helpful?

  2. K

    Warwickshire1 review

    5.0

    Dined 5 days ago

    1. Overall 5
    2. Food 5
    3. Service 5
    4. Ambience 5
    We had a great meal celebratory meal on Saturday - staff were really attentive and the food was just as good as we had experienced previously. Would definitely recommend!

    Is this helpful?

  3. T

    Tom Gold

    Calgary9 reviews

    4.0

    Dined on June 14, 2026

    1. Overall 4
    2. Food 5
    3. Service 5
    4. Ambience 4
    Great host, great waiter and great food. Only complaint is that it was a little too loud.

    Is this helpful?

  4. S

    Shannon

    Calgary6 reviews

    4.0

    Dined on June 13, 2026

    1. Overall 4
    2. Food 3
    3. Service 5
    4. Ambience 4
    We had the steak frites and the steak wasn’t good at all. Very disappointing for price.
    Pigeonhole - 17th AveResponded on June 16, 2026
    Hi Shannon, Thank you for taking some time to write a review for us about your experience with us at Pigeonhole 17th. I'm disheartened to hear that your time with us wasn't enjoyable on account of the steak. I'd love to know a little bit more about what you did not like about our steak offering so that I may provide that information to our chef team. I will send you my email address in a private note. I'd also like to invite you back and offer you an appetizer and entree of your choice as an opportunity to show you what value we can provide. Thank you again for writing in, guest feedback is one of the few ways we can grow as a restaurant. Reshma

    Is this helpful?

  5. R

    Ron

    Calgary1 review

    5.0

    Dined on June 5, 2026

    1. Overall 5
    2. Food 5
    3. Service 5
    4. Ambience 5
    Excellent food, excellent cocktails! Music volume could have been a bit lower, but this is a minor quibble. Would go there again tonight.

    Is this helpful?

  6. T

    Tracey

    Vancouver4 reviews

    5.0

    Dined on June 5, 2026

    1. Overall 5
    2. Food 5
    3. Service 5
    4. Ambience 5
    An terrific dining experience from start to finish! The food was outstanding both in flavour and beautiful presentations. The beet salad was fresh, vibrant, and perfectly balanced, while the cavatelli pasta was simply out of this world. The cocktail program is a delight featuring thoughtfully crafted drinks and a well curated wine list. The service was attentive without being intrusive, warm, and clearly passionate about what they do. They ensured our business dinner was set in the quietest part of the restaurant which was greatly appreciated. This is a fun yet elegant room that's comfortable and inviting. I would gladly return and highly recommend.

    Is this helpful?

    Music was noise. Staff was professional. Interior - what I liked there. Dishes are pricey (my opinion). Interesting to discover this place, not for romantic dates though ☺️

Featured in

  • June 18, 2026

    The Best Restaurants in Calgary

    Calgary is home to some of the top restaurants in Canada. There are several traditional restaurants that have been part of its food scene for decades. These restaurants have a faithful following and serve recipes that have stood the test of time. Known as Stampede City or Cowtown, this city has its share of steakhouses and burger joints. Some are traditional while others have a more modern take on the world of beef. In Calgary, chic restaurants including sushi bars, cocktail lounges and stylish cafes are popping up all around Canada’s Rocky Mountains. Many of the newer restaurants offer special menus for vegetarians or gluten-free diners. The farm-to-table dining experience which focuses on the use of local produce in the recipes is another popular trend with Calgary’s newest dining establishments. There are also hip clubs and lounges in Calgary where diners can let their hair down. Stop by one of these to grab a drink, a late-night snack or a sweet dessert after going to a game or a movie. Some of these clubs often host live bands and musical performers on the weekend. Follow them on social media to keep up with their event schedule. Whether diners are looking for casual dining or wants to dress up for a fancy, romantic dinner with that special someone, Calgary has a restaurant to fit the occasion.

    Published by OpenTable

  • June 18, 2026

    Where Chefs Eat in Calgary

    You’re in luck. The world’s best chefs are sharing their favorite places to dine out in Calgary. We’ve partnered with Phaidon’s Where Chefs Eat, the best-selling restaurant guidebook with over 7,000 recommendations across the globe, to create a list of restaurants in Calgary where chefs love to eat. Grab your copy of this must-have guide, and get 30% off, just for being an OpenTable diner.

    Published by OpenTable

  • June 18, 2026

    86 Calgary / Southern Alberta Restaurants to Love This Valentine's Day

    Check out OpenTable’s suggestions for the best Valentine’s Day restaurant to impress that special someone with a romantic dinner.

    Published by OpenTable

  • June 18, 2026

    The Diner Reviews Are In: 100 Best Restaurants in Canada

    According to OpenTable Diners

    Published by OpenTable

FAQs

Early evening on weekdays or weekend brunch are great bets for a slightly calmer vibe. Guests recommend earlier dinner seatings if you prefer a quieter table, music and energy ramp up later in the night. If you want the lively bar and record-player atmosphere, prime time on Friday/Saturday after 8 pm delivers the full buzz.

Yes, Pigeonhole is a popular and cozy choice for date night. Guests consistently praise the creative small plates, standout dishes (charred cabbage, mushroom croquettes, beef tartare) and attentive service, which make sharing plates and trying cocktails fun. The midcentury, vinyl-driven vibe and dim, buzzy atmosphere suit a lively date, though music can get loud later in the evening, consider an earlier booking if you prefer quieter conversation. Parking is available in a private lot on 17th Ave.

Portions are generally shareable and generous, Pigeonhole’s menu is built for sharing plates and groups. Guests often mention large, shareable mains (half chicken schnitzel, steak frites, pork belly) and small plates that are great for sampling. A few guests found some items small for the price, but most describe portions as ample when you order a mix of small plates and a large plate to share.

Warm, buzzy and eclectic, Pigeonhole mixes midcentury decor, taxidermy accents and a rotating vinyl playlist for a cozy yet energetic vibe. Guests love the personable, service-driven atmosphere and the playful bar energy. Note: several guests mention the music can be loud, especially later in the evening, so it’s lively rather than hushed fine dining.

Choose based on what you want: for energetic nightlife and cocktails, Friday or Saturday delivers the full buzz; for a more relaxed meal or brunch, Saturday or Sunday brunch seatings are popular and slightly calmer earlier in the day. Many guests recommend weekday early evenings for quieter dining and easier conversation.

The posted hours don’t specify public holiday schedules. Guests frequently mention weekend and special-event dining, but for public holidays you should call the restaurant or check their website or social channels to confirm hours and any special holiday service before visiting.

Yes, guests frequently report excellent, personalized service (celebration acknowledgements, dietary guidance, seating requests). The team has been known to call to confirm reservations and ask if parties are celebrating anything. For seating, dietary needs or celebration extras, call the restaurant ahead of your visit so staff can accommodate.

Casual dress is appropriate, the restaurant lists "Casual Dress" and guests commonly dine in smart-casual attire. The space suits date nights, celebrations, and relaxed evenings at the bar. If you’re celebrating something special, guests report staff often go above and beyond to make the night memorable.

Busiest: Friday and Saturday evenings and prime dinner times, plus popular weekend brunch service. Quietest: early weekday evenings and earlier brunch seatings. Guests frequently note lively late-night energy on weekends and recommend earlier reservations if you prefer a quieter meal or better conversation (music often gets louder as the night progresses).

Yes, families and groups do dine here frequently, and guests report family celebrations, birthday parties and teens being welcome. Seating is cozy and shareable-plate friendly; several guests mention bringing larger family groups (including a family of eight). If you need specific accessible seating or space for a stroller, call ahead so the team can accommodate your needs.
